title: Lazard Global Total Return and Income Fund Declares Monthly Distribution and Issues Estimated Sources of the Distribution Announced in April
author: Prnewswire
description: Lazard Global Total Return and Income Fund (LGI) announced a monthly distribution of $0.15340 per share, payable on June 23, 2026. The distribution includes net investment income, realized capital gains, and return of capital. The fund reported a 5-year average annual total return of 5.76% and an annualized distribution rate of 10.30% as of April 30, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: As the parent company of Lazard Asset Management (the fund's investment manager), Lazard Inc is mentioned in a routine operational context. The disclosure that LAM manages $275.4 billion in client assets is informational but does not indicate any material change in business performance or outlook.

title: Lazard Global Total Return and Income Fund Declares Monthly Distribution and Issues Estimated Sources of the Distribution Announced in March
author: Prnewswire
description: Lazard Global Total Return and Income Fund (LGI) announced a monthly distribution of $0.15340 per share payable on May 22, 2026. The distribution consists primarily of return of capital (70%), with smaller portions from net realized long-term capital gains (22%), net realized short-term capital gains (15%), and net investment income (3%). The fund reported a cumulative fiscal year return of -6.86% through March 31, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: As the parent company of Lazard Asset Management (the fund's investment manager), Lazard Inc is mentioned in a factual capacity regarding corporate structure. The fund's underperformance does not directly reflect on the parent company's overall operations or asset management capabilities across its broader business.

title: Lazard Global Total Return and Income Fund Declares Monthly Distribution and Issues Estimated Sources of the Distribution Announced in February
author: Prnewswire
description: Lazard Global Total Return and Income Fund (LGI) declared a monthly distribution of $0.15340 per share, payable on April 22, 2026. The distribution consists of 12% net investment income, 16% net realized long-term capital gains, and 72% return of capital. The fund reported an annualized distribution rate of 9.85% as of February 28, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: As the parent company of Lazard Asset Management (the fund's investment manager), Lazard Inc is mentioned in a routine fund distribution announcement. The news reflects normal fund operations with no material impact on the parent company's business or performance.

title: Lazard Global Total Return and Income Fund Declares Monthly Distribution and Issues Estimated Sources of the Distribution Announced in January
author: Prnewswire
description: Lazard Global Total Return and Income Fund (LGI) announced a monthly distribution of $0.15340 per share, payable on March 23, 2026. The distribution consists primarily of return of capital (65%) and long-term capital gains (32%), with minimal net investment income (3%). The fund reported a 5-year average annual total return of 8.46% relative to NAV and an annualized distribution rate of 9.76%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: As the parent company of Lazard Asset Management (the fund's investment manager), Lazard Inc is mentioned in a neutral context. The announcement reflects routine fund operations and asset management activities. LAM manages $267.0 billion in assets as of January 31, 2026, which is a stable operational metric with no negative or positive implications from this announcement.

title: Lazard Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.50 per Share
author: Prnewswire
description: Lazard Inc. (NYSE: LAZ) announced that its Board of Directors has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.50 per share on outstanding common stock. The dividend is payable on February 20, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 9, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The declaration of a quarterly dividend demonstrates financial strength and confidence in the company's cash flow generation. Dividend payments are generally viewed positively by investors as they represent a return of capital and indicate management's confidence in future earnings.
